seoTitle: How to Embed Typeform in a Website
metaDescription: Learn how to embed a Typeform into any website using HTML or a website builder, with inline, popup, and side tab display options.
intro: Embedding a form directly into your website keeps visitors engaged instead of sending them to a separate page. This guide covers how to generate the Typeform embed code and add it to any site using HTML or a website builder, along with the different display styles available.

steps.heading: Create or open your Typeform
steps.body: Build the form you plan to embed, or open an existing one that's ready to publish on your site.

steps.heading: Copy the embed code
steps.body: Get the embed code from Typeform's share settings so you can add the form to your website.

steps.heading: Add Typeform to your website
steps.body: Paste the embed code using HTML, your CMS, or a website builder's embed block, choosing where on the page it should appear.

faq.question: What display options are available for embedding Typeform?
faq.answer: You can choose from inline embeds, popups, side tabs, and full-page forms depending on how you want the form to appear on your site.

faq.question: Do I need to know HTML to embed a Typeform?
faq.answer: Basic HTML knowledge helps if you're pasting the code manually, but many website builders and CMS platforms support adding it through a simple embed block.

faq.question: Can I embed the same form on multiple pages of my site?
faq.answer: Yes, the same embed code can be reused across different pages wherever you want the form to appear.

faq.question: Will embedding a Typeform slow down my website?
faq.answer: The form loads from Typeform's own servers, so the impact on your site's performance is generally minor, though it's worth testing your page load speed.
